summary: Entropy scan of stdin-only CLI refactor (ad3698d/185ebe5/44c12c4): no dead --data/--file handling remains in write commands; allowed residuals (event/context/packet) confirmed. Tests pass. Found one dead-code pattern, one duplication opportunity, one convention drift (decision.ts), and a small example-string duplication. None block release; all debt-level.
concerns: [low] Dead code: 'err instanceof SyntaxError' branches in phase.ts:105, evolution.ts:69+116, recovery.ts:65 are unreachable — readInputData() wraps SyntaxError into .code==='INVALID_JSON' which is already handled one branch above. Residue from pre-refactor JSON.parse pattern. Debt.

[normal] Duplication: 10-line try/catch-NO_STDIN/INVALID_JSON wrapper around readInputData() is copy-pasted across all 7 write commands. Extract shared helper readJsonInputOrFail(operation, fileErrCtx) in lib/input.ts. Halves boilerplate, removes drift risk. Debt.

[normal] Convention drift: decision.ts uses an inner try/catch around readInputData() despite having no --set fallback, while mission/phase/evolution/recovery use the simpler outer-catch pattern. Only evidence.ts and task.ts record-add need the inner pattern. Align decision.ts with the outer-catch convention. Debt.

[low] Minor duplication: NO_STDIN example strings exist in both input.ts NO_STDIN_EXAMPLES and output.ts noStdinError.examples with slightly divergent formatting. Consolidate into one exported constant. Debt.
